front: Movement: also called the Puteaux Group; met at the home of the Duchamp brothers (Marcel and Jacques)
Movement: name derives from the golden ratio (phi); Jacques Villon suggested the name
Movement: lacked Braque and Picasso's exclusive deal with Daniel Kahnweiler, so exhibited in public salons instead
Movement: Josephin Peladan coined the primary name for this group due to his fascination with phi
Movement: Francis Picabia's Dances at the Spring was shown at this group's 1912 exhibition and at the Armory Show
Movement: Albert Gleizes and Jean Metzinger co-authored 'Du Cubisme,' the first major theoretical treatise on Cubism
Painting: Gleizes's work depicting a man with a golden hunting horn and a group of hunting dogs to his right
Painting: Metzinger's Crystal Cubism work showing a central poilu playing chess against a blue background, with '24' on his collar
back: Soldier at a Game of Chess (Jean Metzinger)

front: Artist: organized the Section d'Or group and designed stained glass windows for the Metz Cathedral; also painted L'Acrobate
work: Key Members: Duchamp Brothers / Jacques Villon

front: Sculpture: large bronze Cubist work by Raymond Duchamp-Villon, cast by his brothers after his death from typhoid fever in WWI
work: Key Members: Duchamp Brothers / Jacques Villon

front: Artist: two Duchamp brothers adopted the surname of this French poet to set themselves apart from the family name
back: François Villon (as adopted by Jacques Villon and Raymond Duchamp-Villon)
work: Key Members: Duchamp Brothers / Jacques Villon

front: Artist: original Section d'Or member and leading Orphist painter of Simultaneous Contrasts: Sun and Moon and Simultaneous Windows on the City
work: Other Members: Robert Delaunay, Francis Picabia, Fernand Léger

front: Artist: Puteaux Group member whose obsession with cylindrical forms led Vauxcelles to nickname his style 'Tubism'
work: Other Members: Robert Delaunay, Francis Picabia, Fernand Léger
